title: madhuca borneensis
intro: Madhuca borneensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Borneo, and is also known as the Borneo butter tree due to its edible fruit pulp. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits. The extracts from the plant have been used to treat various health issues, such as wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Traditional herbal preparations involve using the plant's leaves and fruit pulp to create infusions, decoctions, and topical applications for medicinal purposes.
